> This is roughly the angular resolution needed to position a 1 m arm to within 1 µm — illustrating why **encoder bit-depth and gear reduction jointly define precision**.

---

## 3.5 Analytical Matrix — Factor-to-KPI Mapping

This matrix maps each REDF to the KPIs of ISO 9283 and the relevant safety standard:

| Effectiveness Factor | Affected KPI | Relevant Safety / Performance Standard |
|:---------------------|:-------------|:---------------------------------------|
| Payload | Pose accuracy under load | ISO 10218-1 (Load handling safety) |
| Reach / Workspace | Path accessibility | ISO 9283 (Test volume) |
| DOF | Dexterity & Singularity avoidance | ISO 10218-2 (Cell layout) |
| Resolution | Theoretical precision floor | IEC 61496 (Sensor resolution) |
| Speed / Acceleration | Cycle time & Stopping distance | ISO 13855 (Safety distances) |
| Settling Time | Throughput at end of motion | ISO 9283 (Stability criterion) |
| Accuracy | Absolute positional error | ISO 9283 §6.2 |
| Repeatability | 3$\sigma$ positional spread | ISO 9283 §6.3 |
| Backlash / Hysteresis | Direction-dependent error | ISO 9283 Annex A |
| IP Rating | Environmental robustness | IEC 60529 |
| MTBF / MTTR | Availability & Uptime | ISO 12100 (Reliability) |

---

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# SECTION 4 — Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Master Block Diagram — Robot Effectiveness Determinants

The following Mermaid block diagram presents the **functional architecture** mapping the four categories of REDFs to robot KPIs and downstream safety/performance outcomes.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A[ROBOT EFFECTIVENESS] --> B[Mechanical Kinematic]
    A --> C[Dynamic Performance]
    A --> D[Precision Metrology]
    A --> E[Environmental Operational]

    B --> B1[Payload]
    B --> B2[Workspace Reach]
    B --> B3[DOF]
    B --> B4[Resolution]
    B --> B5[Joint Type]

    C --> C1[Speed]
    C --> C2[Acceleration]
    C --> C3[Cycle Time]
    C --> C4[Settling Time]

    D --> D1[Accuracy]
    D --> D2[Repeatability]
    D --> D3[Drift]
    D --> D4[Backlash]

    E --> E1[IP Rating]
    E --> E2[Temperature]
    E --> E3[Humidity]
    E --> E4[EMI Noise]
    E --> E5[MTBF MTTR]

    B1 --> KPI[ISO 9283 KPIs: AP RP AT RT t]
    B2 --> KPI
    B3 --> KPI
    B4 --> KPI
    C1 --> KPI
    C2 --> KPI
    C3 --> KPI
    C4 --> KPI
    D1 --> KPI
    D2 --> KPI
    D3 --> KPI
    D4 --> KPI
    E1 --> SAF[ISO 10218 SAFETY]
    E2 --> SAF
    E3 --> SAF
    E4 --> SAF
    E5 --> SAF

    KPI --> OUT1[Throughput ROI]
    KPI --> OUT2[Quality Yield]
    SAF --> OUT3[Worker Safety]
    SAF --> OUT4[Compliance Audit]

    style A fill:#1f4e79,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    style B fill:#2e75b6,color:#fff
    style C fill:#2e75b6,color:#fff
    style D fill:#2e75b6,color:#fff
    style E fill:#2e75b6,color:#fff
    style KPI fill:#c00000,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    style SAF fill:#c00000,color:#fff,stroke:#000
    style OUT1 fill:#548235,color:#fff
    style OUT2 fill:#548235,color:#fff
    style OUT3 fill:#548235,color:#fff
    style OUT4 fill:#548235,color:#fff
```

---

## 4.2 Decision Flow — Selecting a Robot Based on Effectiveness Factors

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    S[Start Task Definition] --> Q1{What is the payload?}
    Q1 --> Q1a[Less than 3 kg]
    Q1 --> Q1b[3 to 50 kg]
    Q1 --> Q1c[Greater than 50 kg]
    Q1a --> R1[SCARA or Delta]
    Q1b --> R2[6 DOF Articulated]
    Q1c --> R3[Heavy Payload Articulated or Gantry]

    R1 --> Q2{Required precision}
    Q2 --> Q2a[Less than 0.05 mm]
    Q2 --> Q2b[0.05 to 0.5 mm]
    Q2 --> Q2c[Greater than 0.5 mm]
    Q2a --> EN1[Cleanroom IP65 plus]
    Q2b --> EN2[Standard IP54]
    Q2c --> EN3[IP44 is sufficient]

    R2 --> Q3{Required cycle time}
    Q3 --> Q3a[Less than 0.5 s]
    Q3 --> Q3b[0.5 to 3 s]

    Q3a --> SP1[Delta or Parallel Kinematic]

    Q3c --> SP3[Standard Articulated]

    R3 --> Q4{Workspace shape}

    Q4 --> Q4c[Swept volume]

    Q4b --> WS2[Cylindrical]
    Q4c --> WS3[Articulated 6 DOF]
```
